div.p: This code system https://fhir.dk.swisstph-mis.ch/matchbox/fhir/CodeSystem/emc-custom-codes-codes defines the following codes:

tr.td: Unique identifier provided or a universal ID, if used in the country
Auto-generated unique identification if used by the implementation
Child's Identity unknown/prefer to remain anonymous
The client's identity is unknown or the client's prefers to remain anonymous
Client's family name or last name
The client's date of birth (DOB), if known, OR the estimated date of birth IF not known (see EmCare.A.DE10)
The client's time of birth (if <1 day old)
Age auto-calculated (presented as number of years, months, days) of the client based on date of birth OR estimated age OR based on auto-calculation from estimaged date of birth
The client and/or Caregiver does not know the date of birth of the client (this is to check whether the age and date of birth are facts or estimations)
Provide an estimated age or estimated date of birth
The client/caregiver will provide an estimated age or estimated date of birth
The Caregiver provides the age in weeks/days/hours
The age of the client is estimated based on information provided by the caregiver
Estimated Date of Birth of the Client
Client's home address or address that the client is consenting to disclose
the child could have been created from the mother therefore a relatedpersom may already exist
The client's primary Caregiver's first name
The client's primary Caregiver's middle name
The client's primary Caregiver's last name
Caregiver wants to receive SMS or other messages regarding the child's visits and health status
The caregiver wants to receive SMS or other messages regarding the client's contacts/visits and health status
This is needed to link the CommunicationRequest to the Caregiver
The relationship of the primary Caregiver to the client
The client's mother and father's vital status
The client's mother and father's vital status
The date and time of the client's visit
Person accompanying child today is the primary Caregiver
The client is at the visit with the primary Caregiver
Person accompanying child today's First Name
First name of person accompanying child today when client has not arrived with the primary Caregiver
Person accompanying child today's Last Name
Last name of person accompanying child today when client has not arrived with the primary Caregiver
Person accompanying child today's contact's phone number
Phone number of the visit Caregiver
Person accompanying child today's Relationship to Client
The relationship of the visit Caregiver to the client
This is the type of visit - planned or unplanned
The reason for the client's consultation
The client's visit is for a well child but the child is also presenting as sick
The client's visit is for a new consultation
Referred by a Health Care Worker or Health Care Facility
The client was referred by a Health Care Worker in the community or another Health Care Facility
The client's axillary temperature in degrees Celsius (temperature taken under the armpit)
The health care worker prefers to take the client's rectal temperature
The client's rectal temperature in degrees Celsius (temperature taken in the rectum)
A thermometer is not available to accurately measure the client's temperature
The client is hot to touch
The client's weight cannot be measured
The client's weight has been estimated - to be used for treatment dosing only
The health care worker would prefer to measure the client using length
The client's height cannot be measured
The client's length cannot be measured
The client's weight for height Z Score
The client's weight for length Z Score
The client's weight for age z score
The client's Mid Upper Arm Circumference in Millimeters (child ≥6 months old)
Visually assess for whether underweight (for drug dose calculation)
The health care worker visually assesses for whether underweight (for drug dosing calculations)
The date when the measurement was taken
The client has obstructed or absent breathing
The client is reported to have had one or more convulsions
Number of convulsions in this illness
The number of convulsion(s) the client is reported to have had in this illness
The client is unconscious or lethargic
Not able to drink or breastfeed
The client has reported not to have been able to drink or breastfeed or currently is not able to drink or breastfeed.
The client is reported to be vomiting everything or vomiting but not everything or no vomiting
The healthcare worker or care giver offers the client fluid to check if the client can drink and to check reactions (oral fluid test)
The client has obstructed or absent breathing
The client is reported to have or has had a cough
Length of time the client is reported to have or has had the cough
The client is reported to have or has had difficulty breathing
The length of time the client has or has had difficulty breathing
The client is reported to have or has had diarrhoea
The length of time the client is reported to have or has had diarrhoea
Blood in the stool in this Illness
The client is reported to have or has had blood in the stool in this Illness
The client is reported to have or has had fever
The length of time the client has or has had fever
Has Fever been present every day since the Fever started?
The client has had fever present every day since the fever started
The client is reported to be refusing to use a limb
The client is reported to have, has had, or appears to be in pain
The client is in a high / low / no malaria risk area
Recent Travel to a High Malaria Risk area
The client has recently travelled to a high Malaria risk area
The client is reported to have had or has an ear problem
The client is reported to have had or has ear pain
The client is reported to have or has had ear discharge
The length of time client has or has had ear discharge
The client is reported to have an eye problem
The client is reported to have or has had a skin problem
The client is reported to have itchy skin
The client has been HIV exposed / infected
The client has stridor in a calm child while the client is at rest
The client has experienced recurrent wheeze
The healthcare worker pinches the skin of the client's abdomen for 1 second and then observes how long the skin takes to return to normal once released
The client is restless and irritable
The client has a stiff neck
The health care worker has seen that client is refusing to use a limb
Warm Tender or Swollen Joint or Bone
The client has a warm tender or swollen joint or bone
The client is reported to have oral sores or mouth ulcers
Obvious Cause of Fever (to determine if Malaria test required)
The health care worker determines if there is an obvious cause of fever (to determine if Malaria test is required) If no obvious cause of fever a malaria test will be prompted
The client has a runny nose
The client has a generalised rash
The client has a measles rash
The client has had measles in the last 3 months
The client has clouding of the cornea
The client has pus draining from the eye
The client has tender swelling behind the ear
Pus Seen Draining from the Ear
The client has pus draining from the ear which has been seen by the health care worker
Pus Seen Draining from the Ear for how long
The length of time the client has or has had pus draining from the ear
The client has pus draining from the eye
The client has clouding of the cornea
Is Clouding of Cornea a new problem
Clouding of the cornea in the client is a new problem
Has Clouding of Cornea previously been treated
Clouding of the cornea has not been previously treated in the client
The client has a generalised or localised skin problem
The client has blisters, sores or pustules
The client has a specific type of skin problem(s)
The client has signs of Severe Impetigo / Folliculitis
The client has an extensive skin problem
The client has a severe skin problem
The client has oral sores or mouth ulcers
Add a Skin or Mouth or Eye Problem
The health care worker would like to add a Skin or Mouth or Eye problem found during the physical exam
The client has severe palmar pallor
The client is in a high / low / no malaria risk area
The client has oedema of both feet
Unique identifier provided or a universal ID, if used in the country
Auto-generated unique identification if used by the implementation
The client's identity is unknown or the client's prefers to remain anonymous
The client's sex is not specified
The age of the client is estimated based on physical evidence
The Caregiver provides the age in weeks/days/hours
Estimated Date of Birth of the Client
The Caregiver is the client's mother
The Caregiver is the client's father
The Caregiver is the client's sibling
The Caregiver is the client's extended family
The Caregiver is the client's legal guardian
The Caregiver is not related to the client
Biological Mother or Father Vital Status unknown
The client had a life threatening emergency
The client's visit is for a sick child consultation
The client's visit is for a well child consultation
The client's visit is for a new consultation
The client's visit is for a follow up consultation
The client has had a cough for less than 14 days
The client has had a cough for 14 days or more
The client has had difficulty breathing for less than 14 days
The client has had difficulty breathing for 14 days or more
The client has had diarrhoea for less than 14 days
The client has had diarrhoea for 14 days or more
The client has had fever for less than 7 days
The client has had fever for 7 days or more
The client is reported to have or has had joint or bone pain
Pain or Difficulty Passing Urine or Crying when Passing Urine
The client is reported to have or has had pain or difficulty passing urine or crying when passing urine
The client is reported to have or is observed to have skin pain or problem
The client is reported to have or has had ear pain
The client is reported to have or has had another type of pain
The client is reported to have no pain
The client is in a high malaria risk area
The client is in a low malaria risk area
The client is in a no malaria risk area
The client has had ear discharge for less than 14 days
The client has had ear discharge for 14 days or more
The client's oxygen saturation is more than or equal to 90 %
The client's oxygen saturation is less than 90 %
The clients oxygen saturation was not measured
The client is reported to have had one convulsion in this illness
The client is reported to have had two or more convulsions in this illness
The client is reported to be vomiting everything
The client is reported to be vomiting but not vomiting everything
The client is completely unable to drink
Unable to perform test - assumes vomits everything/unable to drink
The health care worker is unable to perform test on the client therefore the health care worker should assume the client vomits everything or is unable to drink
The client passes the fluid test
Skin Pinch Goes back Very slowly (More than 2 seconds)
The client's skin goes back very slowly after skin pinch of abdomen (More than 2 seconds)
Skin Pinch Goes back Slowly (2 seconds or fewer, but not immediately)
The client's skin goes back slowly after skin pinch of abdomen (2 seconds or fewer, but not immediately)
The client's skin goes back normally after skin pinch of abdomen (immediately)
The client has Skin Lesions ≥ 4 cm
The client has red skin streaks
The client has tender nodes (nodules) under the skin that can appear on any part of the face or body
The client has skin infection that extends to muscle
Molluscum Contagiosum - Skin coloured pearly white papules with central umbilication. Most commonly seen on face and trunk in children.
The client has Molluscum Contagiosum - Skin coloured pearly white papules with central umbilication. Most commonly seen on face and trunk in children.
Warts - Papules or nodules with a rough (Verrucous) surface
The client has Warts - Papules or nodules with a rough (Verrucous) surface
Seborrhea - Greasy scales and redness on central face and body folds
The client has Seborrhea - Greasy scales and redness on central face and body folds
Fixed Drug Reactions - Generalised red, wide spread with small bumps or blisters; or one or more dark skin areas
The client has Fixed Drug Reactions - Generalised red, wide spread with small bumps or blisters; or one or more dark skin areas
Eczema - Wet oozing sores or excoriated, thick patches
The client has Eczema - Wet oozing sores or excoriated, thick patches
Steven Johnson Syndrome - Severe reaction due to cotrimoxazole or NVP involving the skin as well as the eyes and the mouth. Might cause difficulty in breathing
The client has Steven Johnson Syndrome - Severe reaction due to cotrimoxazole or NVP involving the skin as well as the eyes and the mouth. Might cause difficulty in breathing
The client has had pus draining from the ear for less than 14 days
The client has had pus draining from the ear for 14 days or more
The client has a generalised skin problem
The client has a localised skin problem
Possible Serious Abscess - Hot Tender Swelling
The client has a possible serious abscess - Hot tender swelling
The client has Cellulitis - Red tender skin
Papular Itching Rash(Prurigo) - Itching rash with small papules and scratch marks. Dark spots with pale centre
The client has Papular Itching Rash(Prurigo) - itching rash with small papules and scratch marks. Dark spots with pale centre
Ringworm (Tinea) - An itchy circular lesion with a raised edge and fine scaly area in the centre with loss of hair. May also be found on body or web on feet
The client has Ringworm (Tinea) - An itchy circular lesion with a raised edge and fine scaly area in the centre with loss of hair. May also be found on body or web on feet
Scabies - Rash and excoriations on torso; burrows in web space and wrists, face spared
The client has Scabies - Rash and excoriations on torso; burrows in web space and wrists, face spared
Chickenpox - Vesicles over body. Vesicles appear progressively over days and form scabs after they rupture
The client has Chickenpox - Vesicles over body. Vesicles appear progressively over days and form scabs after they rupture
Herpes Zoster - Vesicles in one area on one side of body with intense pain or scars plus shooting pain. Uncommon in children except where they are immuno-compromised (e.g. if infected with HIV)

The client has Herpes Zoster - Vesicles in one area on one side of body with intense pain or scars plus shooting pain. Uncommon in children except where they are immuno-compromised (e.g. if infected with HIV)
Impetigo / Folliculitis - Red, Tender, Warm Crusts or Small lesions
The client has Impetigo / Folliculitis - Red, Tender, Warm Crusts or Small lesions
Mouth Sores or Mouth Ulcers - Deep and Extensive
The client has mouth sores or mouth ulcers that are deep and extensive
Mouth Sores or Mouth Ulcers - Not Deep and Extensive
The client has mouth sores or mouth ulcers that are not deep and extensive
The health care worker would like to add a skin problem
The health care worker would like to add an Oral Sores or Mouth Ulcers problem
The health care worker would like to add an Eye problem
The client has some palmar pallor
The client has no palmar pallor
The client is in a high malaria risk area
The client is in a low malaria risk area
The client is in a no malaria risk area

description.value: Set of codes identified as being needed but not found in existing Code Systems

definition.value: Unique identifier provided or a universal ID, if used in the country
Auto-generated unique identification if used by the implementation

display.value: Child's Identity unknown/prefer to remain anonymous

definition.value: The client's identity is unknown or the client's prefers to remain anonymous
Client's first name
Client's middle name
Client's family name or last name
The client's date of birth (DOB), if known, OR the estimated date of birth IF not known (see EmCare.A.DE10)
The client's time of birth (if <1 day old)
Age auto-calculated (presented as number of years, months, days) of the client based on date of birth OR estimated age OR based on auto-calculation from estimaged date of birth
The client and/or Caregiver does not know the date of birth of the client (this is to check whether the age and date of birth are facts or estimations)

display.value: Provide an estimated age or estimated date of birth

definition.value: The client/caregiver will provide an estimated age or estimated date of birth
The Caregiver provides the age in weeks/days/hours
The age of the client is estimated based on information provided by the caregiver
Estimated Date of Birth of the Client
The Sex of the client
Client's home address or address that the client is consenting to disclose
the child could have been created from the mother therefore a relatedpersom may already exist
The client's primary Caregiver's first name
The client's primary Caregiver's middle name
The client's primary Caregiver's last name
Client's Caregiver's mobile telephone number
Client's Caregiver's home telephone number
Client's Caregiver's work telephone number

display.value: Caregiver wants to receive SMS or other messages regarding the child's visits and health status

definition.value: The caregiver wants to receive SMS or other messages regarding the client's contacts/visits and health status
This is needed to link the CommunicationRequest to the Caregiver
The relationship of the primary Caregiver to the client
The client's mother and father's vital status
The client's mother and father's vital status
The date and time of the client's visit

display.value: Person accompanying child today is the primary Caregiver

definition.value: The client is at the visit with the primary Caregiver
Participant type Person accompanying child
Person accompanying child today

display.value: Person accompanying child today's First Name

definition.value: First name of person accompanying child today when client has not arrived with the primary Caregiver

display.value: Person accompanying child today's Last Name

definition.value: Last name of person accompanying child today when client has not arrived with the primary Caregiver

display.value: Person accompanying child today's contact's phone number

definition.value: Phone number of the visit Caregiver

display.value: Person accompanying child today's Relationship to Client

definition.value: The relationship of the visit Caregiver to the client
This is the type of visit - planned or unplanned
The reason for the client's consultation
The client's visit is for a well child but the child is also presenting as sick
The client's visit is for a new consultation

display.value: Referred by a Health Care Worker or Health Care Facility

definition.value: The client was referred by a Health Care Worker in the community or another Health Care Facility
The client's axillary temperature in degrees Celsius (temperature taken under the armpit)
The health care worker prefers to take the client's rectal temperature
The client's rectal temperature in degrees Celsius (temperature taken in the rectum)
A thermometer is not available to accurately measure the client's temperature
The client is hot to touch
The client's weight in Kilograms
The client's weight cannot be measured
The client's weight has been estimated - to be used for treatment dosing only
The client's height in Centimeters
The health care worker would prefer to measure the client using length
The client's height cannot be measured
The client's length in Centimeters
The client's length cannot be measured
The client's weight for height Z Score
The client's weight for length Z Score
The client's weight for age z score
The client's Mid Upper Arm Circumference in Millimeters (child ≥6 months old)

display.value: Visually assess for whether underweight (for drug dose calculation)

definition.value: The health care worker visually assesses for whether underweight (for drug dosing calculations)
The date when the measurement was taken
The client has obstructed or absent breathing
The client is convulsing now
The client is reported to have had one or more convulsions

display.value: Number of convulsions in this illness

definition.value: The number of convulsion(s) the client is reported to have had in this illness
The client is unconscious or lethargic

display.value: Not able to drink or breastfeed

definition.value: The client has reported not to have been able to drink or breastfeed or currently is not able to drink or breastfeed.
The client is reported to be vomiting everything or vomiting but not everything or no vomiting
The healthcare worker or care giver offers the client fluid to check if the client can drink and to check reactions (oral fluid test)
The client has obstructed or absent breathing
The client is convulsing now
The client is reported to have or has had a cough
Length of time the client is reported to have or has had the cough
The client is reported to have or has had difficulty breathing
The length of time the client has or has had difficulty breathing
The client is reported to have or has had diarrhoea
The length of time the client is reported to have or has had diarrhoea

display.value: Blood in the stool in this Illness

definition.value: The client is reported to have or has had blood in the stool in this Illness
The client is reported to have or has had fever
The length of time the client has or has had fever

display.value: Has Fever been present every day since the Fever started?

definition.value: The client has had fever present every day since the fever started
The client is reported to be refusing to use a limb
The client is reported to have, has had, or appears to be in pain
The client is in a high / low / no malaria risk area

display.value: Recent Travel to a High Malaria Risk area

definition.value: The client has recently travelled to a high Malaria risk area
The client is reported to have had or has an ear problem
The client is reported to have had or has ear pain
The client is reported to have or has had ear discharge
The length of time client has or has had ear discharge
The client is reported to have an eye problem
The client is reported to have or has had a skin problem
The client is reported to have itchy skin
The client's respiratory rate
The client has chest indrawing
The client has been HIV exposed / infected
The client has stridor in a calm child while the client is at rest
The client is wheezing
The client has experienced recurrent wheeze
The client's oxygen saturation measurement
The client has sunken eyes
The healthcare worker pinches the skin of the client's abdomen for 1 second and then observes how long the skin takes to return to normal once released
The client is restless and irritable
The client has a stiff neck
The health care worker has seen that client is refusing to use a limb

display.value: Warm Tender or Swollen Joint or Bone

definition.value: The client has a warm tender or swollen joint or bone
The client is reported to have oral sores or mouth ulcers

display.value: Obvious Cause of Fever (to determine if Malaria test required)

definition.value: The health care worker determines if there is an obvious cause of fever (to determine if Malaria test is required) If no obvious cause of fever a malaria test will be prompted
The client has a runny nose
The client has red eyes
The client has a generalised rash
The client has a measles rash
The client has had measles in the last 3 months
The client has clouding of the cornea
The client has pus draining from the eye
The client has tender swelling behind the ear

display.value: Pus Seen Draining from the Ear

definition.value: The client has pus draining from the ear which has been seen by the health care worker

display.value: Pus Seen Draining from the Ear for how long

definition.value: The length of time the client has or has had pus draining from the ear
The client has pus draining from the eye
The client has clouding of the cornea

display.value: Is Clouding of Cornea a new problem

definition.value: Clouding of the cornea in the client is a new problem

display.value: Has Clouding of Cornea previously been treated

definition.value: Clouding of the cornea has not been previously treated in the client
The client has a generalised or localised skin problem
The client has blisters, sores or pustules
The client has a specific type of skin problem(s)
The client has signs of Severe Impetigo / Folliculitis
The client has an extensive skin problem
The client has a severe skin problem
The client has oral sores or mouth ulcers

display.value: Add a Skin or Mouth or Eye Problem

definition.value: The health care worker would like to add a Skin or Mouth or Eye problem found during the physical exam
The client has severe palmar pallor
The client is in a high / low / no malaria risk area
The client has oedema of both feet
Unique identifier provided or a universal ID, if used in the country
Auto-generated unique identification if used by the implementation
The client's identity is unknown or the client's prefers to remain anonymous
The client is Female
The client is Male
The client's sex is not specified
The age of the client is estimated based on physical evidence
The Caregiver provides the age in weeks/days/hours
Estimated Date of Birth of the Client
Client's Caregiver's mobile telephone number
Client's Caregiver's home telephone number
Client's Caregiver's work telephone number
The Caregiver is the client's mother
The Caregiver is the client's father
The Caregiver is the client's sibling
The Caregiver is the client's extended family
The Caregiver is the client's legal guardian
The Caregiver is not related to the client
Biological Mother or Father Vital Status unknown
Biological Mother or Father Alive
Biological Mother or Father dead
The visit is planned
The visit is unplanned
The client had a life threatening emergency
The client's visit is for a sick child consultation
The client's visit is for a well child consultation
The client's visit is for a new consultation
The client's visit is for a follow up consultation
The client has had a cough for less than 14 days
The client has had a cough for 14 days or more
The client has had difficulty breathing for less than 14 days
The client has had difficulty breathing for 14 days or more
The client has had diarrhoea for less than 14 days
The client has had diarrhoea for 14 days or more
The client has had fever for less than 7 days
The client has had fever for 7 days or more
The client is reported to have or has had joint or bone pain

display.value: Pain or Difficulty Passing Urine or Crying when Passing Urine

definition.value: The client is reported to have or has had pain or difficulty passing urine or crying when passing urine
The client is reported to have or is observed to have skin pain or problem
The client is reported to have or has had ear pain
The client is reported to have or has had another type of pain
The client is reported to have no pain
The client is in a high malaria risk area
The client is in a low malaria risk area
The client is in a no malaria risk area
The client has had ear discharge for less than 14 days
The client has had ear discharge for 14 days or more
The client's oxygen saturation is more than or equal to 90 %
The client's oxygen saturation is less than 90 %
The clients oxygen saturation was not measured
The client is reported to have had one convulsion in this illness
The client is reported to have had two or more convulsions in this illness
The client is reported to be vomiting everything
The client is reported to be vomiting but not vomiting everything
The client vomits immediately / everything
The client is completely unable to drink
The client drinks poorly
The client drinks eagerly / thirstily
The client drinks normally

display.value: Unable to perform test - assumes vomits everything/unable to drink

definition.value: The health care worker is unable to perform test on the client therefore the health care worker should assume the client vomits everything or is unable to drink
The client passes the fluid test

display.value: Skin Pinch Goes back Very slowly (More than 2 seconds)

definition.value: The client's skin goes back very slowly after skin pinch of abdomen (More than 2 seconds)

display.value: Skin Pinch Goes back Slowly (2 seconds or fewer, but not immediately)

definition.value: The client's skin goes back slowly after skin pinch of abdomen (2 seconds or fewer, but not immediately)
The client's skin goes back normally after skin pinch of abdomen (immediately)
The client has Skin Lesions ≥ 4 cm
The client has red skin streaks
The client has tender nodes (nodules) under the skin that can appear on any part of the face or body
The client has skin infection that extends to muscle

display.value: Molluscum Contagiosum - Skin coloured pearly white papules with central umbilication. Most commonly seen on face and trunk in children.

definition.value: The client has Molluscum Contagiosum - Skin coloured pearly white papules with central umbilication. Most commonly seen on face and trunk in children.

display.value: Warts - Papules or nodules with a rough (Verrucous) surface

definition.value: The client has Warts - Papules or nodules with a rough (Verrucous) surface

display.value: Seborrhea - Greasy scales and redness on central face and body folds

definition.value: The client has Seborrhea - Greasy scales and redness on central face and body folds

display.value: Fixed Drug Reactions - Generalised red, wide spread with small bumps or blisters; or one or more dark skin areas

definition.value: The client has Fixed Drug Reactions - Generalised red, wide spread with small bumps or blisters; or one or more dark skin areas

display.value: Eczema - Wet oozing sores or excoriated, thick patches

definition.value: The client has Eczema - Wet oozing sores or excoriated, thick patches

display.value: Steven Johnson Syndrome - Severe reaction due to cotrimoxazole or NVP involving the skin as well as the eyes and the mouth. Might cause difficulty in breathing

definition.value: The client has Steven Johnson Syndrome - Severe reaction due to cotrimoxazole or NVP involving the skin as well as the eyes and the mouth. Might cause difficulty in breathing
The client has had pus draining from the ear for less than 14 days
The client has had pus draining from the ear for 14 days or more
The client has a generalised skin problem
The client has a localised skin problem

display.value: Possible Serious Abscess - Hot Tender Swelling

definition.value: The client has a possible serious abscess - Hot tender swelling
The client has Cellulitis - Red tender skin

display.value: Papular Itching Rash(Prurigo) - Itching rash with small papules and scratch marks. Dark spots with pale centre

definition.value: The client has Papular Itching Rash(Prurigo) - itching rash with small papules and scratch marks. Dark spots with pale centre

display.value: Ringworm (Tinea) - An itchy circular lesion with a raised edge and fine scaly area in the centre with loss of hair. May also be found on body or web on feet

definition.value: The client has Ringworm (Tinea) - An itchy circular lesion with a raised edge and fine scaly area in the centre with loss of hair. May also be found on body or web on feet

display.value: Scabies - Rash and excoriations on torso; burrows in web space and wrists, face spared

definition.value: The client has Scabies - Rash and excoriations on torso; burrows in web space and wrists, face spared

display.value: Chickenpox - Vesicles over body. Vesicles appear progressively over days and form scabs after they rupture

definition.value: The client has Chickenpox - Vesicles over body. Vesicles appear progressively over days and form scabs after they rupture

display.value: Herpes Zoster - Vesicles in one area on one side of body with intense pain or scars plus shooting pain. Uncommon in children except where they are immuno-compromised (e.g. if infected with HIV)

definition.value: The client has Herpes Zoster - Vesicles in one area on one side of body with intense pain or scars plus shooting pain. Uncommon in children except where they are immuno-compromised (e.g. if infected with HIV)

display.value: Impetigo / Folliculitis - Red, Tender, Warm Crusts or Small lesions

definition.value: The client has Impetigo / Folliculitis - Red, Tender, Warm Crusts or Small lesions
The client has oral thrush

display.value: Mouth Sores or Mouth Ulcers - Deep and Extensive

definition.value: The client has mouth sores or mouth ulcers that are deep and extensive

display.value: Mouth Sores or Mouth Ulcers - Not Deep and Extensive

definition.value: The client has mouth sores or mouth ulcers that are not deep and extensive
The health care worker would like to add a skin problem
The health care worker would like to add an Oral Sores or Mouth Ulcers problem
The health care worker would like to add an Eye problem
The client has palmar pallor
The client has some palmar pallor
The client has no palmar pallor
The client is in a high malaria risk area
The client is in a low malaria risk area
The client is in a no malaria risk area
